data_sources: Granola (queried — partial results for CLS, GLOC, Plymouth Bay; Slack used as primary source)

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — no response since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; contact was not the decision maker

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Deal broken off by Solv 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; no viable integration path

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free during pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay usage at scale; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition; ~2K calls/week; starting with 2 locations
deals.status_narrative: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27. Jim (CEO) drove the deal across the finish line after a multi-week pricing and ROI process. Pricing locked: free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale. Salesforce is being updated and implementation kickoff is next. The org recently 3x'd via two acquisitions (14 → 40 physicians, 20 → 100 PAs). Per Granola: Jim's email is jstilley@gogloc.com.
key_risks: Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) still unproven at scale
No ortho reference customer — Jim flagged this throughout but softened over time
Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity is elevated
deals.next_step: Brantley to button up Salesforce and kick off implementation onboarding. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: SIGNED 4/27. Kicking off implementation — Salesforce update in progress.

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay; 250 providers total; Texas; 15–30 providers and ~15–30 telemed visits/day to start
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager, technical lead); Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical champion); CLS President (executive sponsor, actively applying pressure to move)
deals.status_narrative: CLS came back 4/24 with three pricing requests framed as 'help us make it easier to sign with Solv because we want to': (1) shift from flat monthly fee to per-encounter or hybrid pricing, (2) shorten initial term from 1 year to 6 months, (3) reduce or waive the $2K implementation fee. They also opened a separate ClearPay design partner conversation for specialty clinics covering auth/referral workflows — they met with pVerify and confirmed pVerify cannot do what they want. Luma also confirmed they cannot do dynamic eligibility, which is Solv's key edge. Critical open question: ECW + SolvPay compatibility — this gates the full deal scope. President is engaged and applying pressure on Abigail's team. June 1 target launch. Per Granola: deal includes 'Uber-style' pooled visit queue, after-hours focus initially (5–9pm), scaling to weekends.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gating the entire deal scope
Pricing concession stack (per-encounter model, 6-month term, waived impl fee) compresses margin and needs finance sign-off
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year — CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Confirm ECW + SolvPay feasibility (Owner: Teresa/Brantley, ASAP). Evaluate and respond to pricing requests (Owner: Katie/Teresa, this week). Respond before CLS president's internal meeting.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Pricing concessions requested 4/24; ECW+SolvPay compatibility is the critical unresolved blocker.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates
deals.size: 1 location active (standalone ortho urgent care, opening June 1); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; per Granola: ~$800/mo/loc + $1K one-time setup discussed
deals.locations: 1 active (urgent care focus); 3 additional ortho clinics in scope for expansion
deals.champion: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, 1yr tenure, decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, 4mo tenure, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user — internally tasked to evaluate Solv)
deals.status_narrative: Strong 4/8 team demo with four stakeholders (Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). No major UX or workflow objections; walk-ins, same-day booking, and queue management all landed well for the urgent care use case. Verbal interest confirmed; team doing internal review with reconnect expected ~1 week from 4/12. That window has now passed with no update in the channel — follow-up is overdue. June 1 opening is a real forcing function. ECW live integration demo is the primary gate — Christopher explicitly wants to see data actually flowing into ECW. Referral workflow gap blocks expansion beyond urgent care to the other 3 ortho clinics.
key_risks: No follow-up since 4/12 — gone quiet after strong team demo; reconnect is overdue
ECW live integration demo is a hard gate — Christopher needs to see data flowing, not just hear about it
Referral workflow gap (HMO referral requirement) blocks expansion to the other 3 ortho clinics
deals.next_step: Brantley to send ECW integration packet (was due Mon/Tues after 4/8 — overdue) and re-engage to schedule pricing/reconnect call. Owner: Brantley. Due: ASAP.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Gone quiet 2+ weeks post-demo; ECW live integration is the hard gate; follow-up overdue.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused — was near Paper Process)
deals.size: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; Athena; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating — proactively communicated the Assort blocker rather than going dark
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that contractual obligations to Assort Health block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — with no timeline given. This deal was previously at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign was ready) before the Assort blocker surfaced 2/25. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley on passive check-in cadence (~every 2 weeks). Last check-in target was ~4/14 — likely overdue.
key_risks: Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path forward
Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure during the wait window
No active outreach risk — if Assort resolves and another vendor moves in first, deal is lost
deals.next_step: Brantley to check in now — targeted 4/14 check-in is overdue. Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract hard block; Jon will re-engage when clear — no timeline; check-in overdue.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off — acquisition-only, 6–9 month hold)
deals.size: 47 locations confirmed; ECW with CTO-built homegrown scheduler (will not be replaced); acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
deals.status_narrative: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in — 'too many resources poured into it' — and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as a patient acquisition layer only (GMB routing to Solv booking) running in parallel with their existing scheduler, with ECW sync for new bookings. Not on their timeline for 6–9 months. Also actively piloting a competing voice agent they declined to name. Team was offered the option to shut down. This is a 'Primary Care Connect' type structure, not standard Monarch fit.
key_risks: 6-9 month self-stated timeline with zero commitment — long, low-probability hold
Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — Solv product appetite for this model is unknown
Competing voice agent pilot already underway with no visibility into outcome
deals.next_step: Internal Solv decision needed: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close out. If pursuing, validate acquisition-layer + ECW integration feasibility first. Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: now.
deals.next_step_timing: Decision needed now; re-engage Q3 2026 if parked
deals.key_notes_one_line: Cut off 4/7; won't replace scheduler; acquisition-only scope; team needs to decide park or close.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only at ~$0.50/visit
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model doesn't land for a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-up requests for a leadership call since 3/24. Real collection pain exists (post-visit statements, IPA routing errors) but the product-fit gap is genuine and owner-level. Team is not pushing hard given Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care.
key_risks: Product-fit mismatch is explicit and owner-level — not a pricing or timing issue
Dark since mid-March — ghosting all follow-ups
Aggressive pursuit risks straining the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership
deals.next_step: One final low-touch follow-up only. Do not push. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves to better fit recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley. No active pursuit.
deals.next_step_timing: No active pursuit; potential re-engagement if ClearPay model evolves
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection; near dead; pushing risks the Mederi partnership.

early_stage_watch.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
early_stage_watch.contact: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former CEO of AFC)
early_stage_watch.notes: Intro via Heather at UCA happy hour. First substantive call 4/24 with Brantley. 4 locations, urgent ortho model, cash-constrained (opened 3 locs at once; Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling badly without MRI for months). Randy meeting with OS1 team mid-week (~4/28–4/30) to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Interested in Maya and ClearPay. Reconnecting late April/early May. Evan Cory helping prepare materials. Explicit flag: they are cash-strapped.

executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Closed Won | Design Partner
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs; ~2K calls/week
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jim Stilley (CEO)
- **Status**: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27. Jim drove it across the finish line after a multi-week pricing and ROI process. Pricing locked at free pilot then usage-based at scale. Salesforce update in progress; implementation kickoff is next.
- **Key risks**:
  - Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim raised this throughout
  - Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity is elevated
- **Next step**: Button up Salesforce and begin implementation onboarding. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo
- **Size**: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; 250 providers total; 15–30 providers and visits/day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR/technical lead); Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical); CLS President engaged and applying pressure
- **Status**: CLS came back 4/24 with three pricing requests framed as wanting to sign: shift to per-encounter or hybrid pricing, shorten to 6-month first term, and waive the $2K implementation fee. ECW+SolvPay compatibility remains unconfirmed and gates the full deal scope. Also opened a separate ClearPay design partner conversation — pVerify and Luma both confirmed they can't do what CLS wants, giving Solv a clear opening. June 1 target launch.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — this gates the entire deal scope
  - Pricing concession stack needs finance sign-off and compresses margin
  - Potential EMR switch to Epic/Oracle in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
- **Next step**: Confirm ECW + SolvPay feasibility (Owner: Teresa/Brantley, ASAP). Respond to pricing requests (Owner: Katie/Teresa, this week).
executive_summaries.plymouth_bay_ortho:
**Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates** — Building Our Champion | New Logo
- **Size**: 1 location (ortho urgent care, June 1 opening); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; ~$800/mo/loc discussed
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, former Solv user, internally tasked to evaluate)
- **Status**: Strong 4/8 team demo — four stakeholders, no major objections, urgent care use case is a clean fit. Team doing internal review; reconnect was expected ~4/19 but no follow-up has appeared in the channel. ECW live integration demo is the primary gate — Christopher explicitly wants to see data actually flowing into ECW.
- **Key risks**:
  - Gone quiet 2+ weeks post-demo — follow-up is overdue
  - ECW live integration demo is a hard gate before they'll commit
  - Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to other 3 ortho clinics
- **Next step**: Brantley to send ECW integration packet and re-engage immediately. Owner: Brantley. Due: ASAP (overdue).
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement with no timeline. Was at near-Paper Process before this. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley's ~4/14 check-in is likely overdue.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled
  - Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure
  - No control over timing from Solv's side
- **Next step**: Brantley to check in now (overdue from ~4/14). Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley.
executive_summaries.total_primary_care:
**Total Primary Care** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off) | New Logo
- **Size**: 47 locations; ECW; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
- **Status**: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as acquisition layer only (GMB → Solv booking in parallel), not on timeline for 6–9 months. Actively piloting a competing voice agent. Team was offered the option to shut down. Non-standard Monarch deal structure.
- **Key risks**:
  - 6-9 month timeline with zero commitment; long low-probability hold
  - Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — internal Solv appetite unknown
  - Competing voice agent pilot underway with no outcome visibility
- **Next step**: Internal Solv decision — park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close out. Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: now.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back explicitly on 3/16 — '90% of patients are recurring, insurances are stable, doesn't make sense.' ClearPay doesn't fit high-recurring primary care. No response since mid-March. Real pain exists but product fit doesn't. Pushing risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership.
- **Key risks**:
  - Product-fit objection is owner-level and substantive — not pricing or timing
  - Dark since mid-March; ghosting all follow-ups
  - Aggressive pursuit risks the Mederi Urgent Care relationship
- **Next step**: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Owner: Brantley.

leadership_table.key_notes: Pricing concessions requested 4/24; ECW+SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gating close.
Gone quiet 2+ weeks post-demo; ECW live integration is the hard gate; follow-up overdue.
Assort contract hard block; Jon intends to return when clear — no timeline.
Cut off 4/7; won't replace scheduler; team must decide to park or close.
Owner's explicit product-fit objection; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.
Very early post-UCA intro; board member contact; reconnecting ~5/1 after internal OS1 meeting.
